meta.reportLabel: Sat Report — JPY follow-through CONFIRMED (past 161, 2nd day, Katayama backing): JPY complex upgraded; Fed Sept hike odds halved to ~53% post-NFP - USD momentum cracking, DXY cut to RANGE; RBNZ Wed re-muddied to a COIN-FLIP (OIS ~70% hike vs bank hold) - NZD/CAD cut to MED; funeral week = oil headline vacuum.
meta.coverage: USD, EUR, GBP, JPY, AUD, NZD, CAD · 18 pairs + DXY + JPYBASKET + GER40
meta.regime: The intervention regime got its confirmation: JPY strengthened past 161 Friday - a second straight day of gains after Thursday's suspected ambush - with FinMin Katayama saying authorities 'stand ready to intervene at any time.' The long-JPY re-load signal has triggered. Beneath it, the +57k NFP miss is biting: Sept Fed-hike odds fell to ~53% (from 60-64%), yields dropped and USD momentum cracked at the margin (still floored by zero-cuts pricing). The oil glut (WTI ~$68.5, Brent ~$70.5, lowest since late Feb) keeps CAD weakest - it lagged even Friday's bounce. RBNZ Wed is a genuine coin-flip; Doha talks paused Jul 4-9 for Khamenei's funeral.
meta.nextBigEvent: Wed Jul 8 double event: coin-flip RBNZ (OIS ~70% hike vs bank-economist hold) + Fed June minutes; funeral-week oil headlines the rolling tail

dailyRead: The yen got its follow-through: after Thursday's suspected 2:30am ambush, JPY strengthened past 161 on Friday - a second consecutive day of gains - with Finance Minister Katayama stating authorities 'stand ready to intervene at any time.' The re-load signal flagged yesterday has TRIGGERED: the JPY complex is upgraded across the board (USD/JPY and CAD/JPY shorts to MEDIUM, JPYBASKET long to MEDIUM). Meanwhile the +57k NFP miss is biting the dollar harder than Thursday suggested - Sept hike odds fell to ~53% (from 60-64%), yields dropped, and USD finished at the bottom of the majors table before stabilizing; Warsh's July 1 'prices are too high' line keeps the hold hawkish, but USD momentum has cracked at the margin (DXY cut to RANGE). The oil glut still pins CAD as clear weakest (WTI ~$68.5 / Brent ~$70.5, lowest since late Feb - CAD lagged even Friday's small pre-weekend bounce, the tell of a structurally offered currency), and RBNZ Wednesday has re-muddied into a genuine coin-flip (OIS still prices ~70% hike; ASB/Westpac/ANZ all forecast hold) - NZD/CAD is cut HIGH->MED on event risk. Cleanest board: USD/CAD and AUD/CAD longs, plus the freshly-armed JPY shorts. Funeral week = five days of frozen diplomacy and gap risk over every oil-linked position.

strength.drivers: CPI 4.2% headline / core 2.9% (May); June NFP +57k big miss, unemp 4.2%; Fed 3.50-3.75% hawkish hold, zero 2026 cuts priced; Sept hike odds fell to ~53% (from 60-64%), yields lower; Warsh Jul 1: 'prices are too high' - no forward guidance.
strength.forward: 🦅⚖️ Hawkish hold into the Jul 28-29 FOMC but the hike tail is halved (Sept ~53%); the zero-cut floor holds USD up while the NFP miss removed the motor. Jun CPI (~Jul 14) either re-arms the tail or confirms the crack. Risk: a second soft labor print unwinds stretched longs. Next FOMC Jul 28-29.

strength.drivers: Core 2.6% (May, up from 2.5%); services CPI 3.7% (up from 3.2%); headline 2.8% held; DMP wages 4.2%. BoE 3.75%, hawkish 7-2 hold (2 hike votes); next BoE Jul 30 + MPR.
strength.forward: 🦅 Hawkish - the services re-acceleration keeps the hike-tail and both dissents alive into the Jul 30 MPR round. Risk: a soft activity/labor wave re-splits the MPC. Next BoE Jul 30.

strength.drivers: Core CPI 1.4% (May, subsidy-suppressed); unemp 2.5%, jobs/applicants 1.26 tight. BoJ 1.00% (hiked Jun 16, 7-1), guides more hikes. JPY past 161 Friday (from the 162.66 low) - second straight day of gains after the suspected ambush; Katayama: ready to intervene 'at any time.'
strength.forward: 🦅 Follow-through CONFIRMED: two days of gains + verbal backing + the no-warning regime + the BoJ hike path (Jul 30-31 next) - long-JPY adds now justified, trail stops rather than pyramid. Risk: a full April-style retrace (¥11.7tn round had two good days too); the ~250bp+ carry gap. Next BoJ Jul 30-31.

strength.drivers: May CPI: trimmed mean 3.6% (up from 3.4%); headline 4.0% (RBA sees a 4.8% Q2 peak); unemp 4.5% loosening. RBA 4.35% (hiked May), held Jun; market prices ~4.70% by year-end.
strength.forward: ⚖️🦅 Sticky trim + year-end pricing keeps an Aug 11 hike tail open; AUD capped sub-0.70 by the oil-glut hit to commodity beta. Risk: cool Q2 jobs/CPI or risk-off flushes the beta. Next RBA Aug 11.

strength.tag: Jul 8: OIS ~70% hike vs bank hold
strength.drivers: CPI 3.1% Q1 (RBNZ sees >4% Q2, 4.3% Q3 peak); unemp 5.3% Q1. RBNZ 2.25%; May was a 3-3 split decided by Governor Breman's casting vote.
strength.forward: ⚖️🦅 RE-MUDDIED: Jul 8 is a genuine coin-flip - OIS still prices ~70%+ of a 25bp hike while ASB/Westpac/ANZ all forecast a hold (hikes from Aug/Sep); 'a closer call than markets expect.' Either way H2 tightening is the base case. Risk: two-way - a dovish-hold tone flushes the priced hike; a hike squeezes NZD sharply higher. Next RBNZ Jul 8.

strength.tag: flash 2.8% cooling BUT core up to 2.5%
strength.drivers: EZ June flash HICP 2.8% (down from 3.2%; energy 8.7% from 10.8%) BUT core ROSE to 2.5% from 2.2%. Unemp 6.3%. ECB 2.25% depo (hiked Jun 11, first since 2023), meeting-by-meeting.
strength.forward: ⚖️ Two-handed flash: the cooling headline guts Jul 23 urgency (hold expected), but rising core keeps a Sept hike alive - the dovish EUR read is not clean. Risk: a hawkish Jul 23 tone on the core print squeezes EUR shorts. Next ECB Jul 23.

strength.drivers: May CPI 3.2% (energy-driven; ex-energy 2.1%, trim ~2.0%); unemp 6.6%; jobs flat since Jan, wages 3.0% cooling; technical recession. BoC 2.25%, 5th straight hold, looking through energy. WTI ~$68.5 / Brent ~$70.5 - lowest since late Feb.
strength.forward: 🔻 The glut deepens and CAD lagged even Friday's pre-weekend oil bounce - the tell of a structurally offered currency; the energy-CPI floor erodes while the terms-of-trade hit deepens; BoC sidelined Jul 15. Risk: a funeral-week Hormuz incident re-spikes oil; a Jul 10 jobs surprise. Next BoC Jul 15.

geopolitics: Funeral week has begun: Khamenei's rites opened July 4 (running through July 9 across Tehran, Qom and Mashhad; 15-20 million mourners expected - the largest state funeral in Iran's history) and the Doha talks are PAUSED until it ends. The concluded round was substantive: two days on Strait of Hormuz maritime traffic and financial incentives for Iran - Axios reports the US is literally negotiating Tehran out of charging TOLLS on the strait, and Tehran still publicly asserts control over it. Oil steadied into the long weekend - WTI ~$68.5, Brent ~$70.5, still the lowest since late February - as traders secured supply pre-holiday; the structural glut is intact (Hormuz >10mn b/d with US escort, UAE 3.9mn b/d restored, Iran 40mn+ bbl shipped, record Russian exports, floating storage building). Transmission unchanged: falling oil -> eroding energy-CPI floors -> less CB hawkishness at the margin -> CAD holds the bag (it lagged even Friday's bounce - the tell). Falling oil remains the GER40 tailwind and does NOT buy a Fed cut (US core runs 2.9%; the disinflation is energy-led). The tail is the vacuum itself: five days of frozen diplomacy, high emotion, and a standing Iranian Hormuz warning - an incident with negotiators sidelined would re-spike oil into a market that has fully crushed the war premium (rescuing CAD, hurting AUD/NZD/GER40, handing USD and JPY competing haven bids). With floating storage high, even a spike likely fades faster than prior episodes.
